decisions.reasoning: Q3 FY26 earnings beat (EPS $4.27 vs $4.07 consensus, revenue $82.9B +18% YoY) shows fundamentals intact. Stock has been in a sustained downtrend from $460 peak on June 1 to $376 today — a 18% decline driven by AI capex ROI concerns and sector rotation, not fundamental deterioration. Fast SMA ($395) below slow SMA ($413), price below both — technically bearish. Next catalyst: Q4 FY26 earnings July 29. Closing here locks in -$147 loss at the trough of a 17-session declining streak with no meaningful near-term catalyst to the downside. Position is small at 11.8% of portfolio. Holding for July 29 earnings recovery thesis.

sources.headline: Microsoft Stock Is Trailing the Market in 2026 — Is It a Buy?
Microsoft Q3 FY2026: Revenue $82.9B +18%, EPS $4.27 vs $4.07 est, Net Income +23%

decisions.invalidation: Break below $370 support on heavy volume with no recovery; Q4 FY26 earnings miss on July 29; material deterioration in Azure growth rate

decisions.reasoning: Q1 FY27 earnings (May 20) massively beat: revenue $81.6B +85% YoY, Data Center $75.2B +92%, EPS $1.87 vs $1.76. Q2 FY27 guidance: $91B at 75% non-GAAP gross margins. Stock hit $199 low on June 10 after June 5 sector selloff triggered by Broadcom AI guidance miss (PHLX chip index -10%). Now consolidating $204-$212 with stabilizing technicals. Fast SMA ($206) approaching slow SMA ($215) from below, price ($207) near fast SMA — early recovery signs. Fundamental story is the strongest in the portfolio; hold for mean reversion toward $220+.

sources.headline: Nvidia Q1 FY27 earnings: $81.6B revenue +85% YoY, EPS $1.87 vs $1.76
Structural Risks to Watch for NVDA Stock Over the Next 6 Months

decisions.invalidation: Break below $199 June low with volume confirmation; new Commerce Dept China embargo; hyperscaler Q2 capex guidance cut below $60B

decisions.reasoning: Position opened June 16 (2 days old — within 3-day no-churn window). Broad tech index providing diversification. Price ($735) recovering above both fast ($719) and slow ($721) SMAs, suggesting trend improvement. No catalyst to reverse.
decisions.invalidation: Sustained break below $720 (SMA cluster); macro shock (FOMC hawkish surprise, CPI miss)

decisions.reasoning: Only profitable position (+1.62%). Broad market near highs; price ($745) above fast ($741) and slow ($744) SMAs — constructive technicals. Serves as portfolio anchor ballast. No reason to trim.
decisions.invalidation: Sustained break below $730 (fast SMA); recession signals

decisions.reasoning: No new positions initiated. Gross long exposure at 91% leaves only ~4pp headroom before hitting the 95% cap. With $853 cash, any meaningful new position would breach the limit. Preserving cash as volatility buffer given MSFT and NVDA in drawdown.

summary: First run. Held all 4 positions; MSFT in downtrend but July 29 earnings thesis intact (-11.5%), NVDA consolidating at support post June 5 sector selloff (-4.3%), SPY only green position (+1.6%). No trades. Day P&L +$90.60 (+0.96%).
open_theses: MSFT: Hold through July 29 Q4 FY26 earnings — earnings beat streak and strong Azure/AI fundamentals justify patience despite downtrend; watch for reversal above $396 fast SMA
NVDA: Consolidating $200-$212 support zone after sector selloff; Q2 FY27 guidance $91B revenue could catalyze rally back toward $220-$230; potential add if price breaks above $215 with cash available
QQQ/SPY: Broad index positions for diversification and market exposure; SPY performing well, QQQ recovering above SMAs
